title: Qualifications
questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'qualification'?
What do you think of qualifications?
What qualifications do you have?
What qualifications would you like to have?
Which qualifications do you think are best to have?
Do you think everybody needs qualifications?
Do all qualifications need to be recognized by employers?
Do you think some qualifications are a waste of time and money?
What are the minimum qualifications needed to do the job you have / want?
What qualifications would you expect your English teacher to have?
Are qualifications important to you?
Would you rather have a well-qualified teacher with little experience or a very experienced teacher with no qualifications?
Do you think everyone should do a qualification in first aid?
Do you think there should be minimum qualifications for politicians and presidents?
What qualifications does your country's leader have for his/her job?
Do you think qualifications should be the same in each country so they can be used all over the world?
What do you think of the idea of a parenting qualification that all future parents must study for?
Have you ever lied (would you ever lie) about the qualifications on your resume in order to get a really good job?
Is it possible to be overqualified for a job (i.e. to have too many qualifications)?
Do all qualifications need to be of the paper variety?
